title: Terrorist attacks by region
subtitle: Number of [terrorist attacks](#dod:terrorism) by world region.
caption: Between 1992 and 2021, the number of terrorist attacks recorded across world regions fluctuated significantly, with the total count rising from roughly 4,000 in the early 1990s to a peak exceeding 16,000 around 2014 before declining to approximately 8,000 by 2021. The stacked area chart displays data for twelve distinct regions, where the vertical axis measures the number of attacks ranging from 0 to 18,000. In the early years of the timeline, specifically 1992, the Middle East & North Africa and South Asia contributed the largest shares to the global total, while regions like Australasia & Oceania and Central America & Caribbean remained near the bottom of the scale with minimal activity. A visible expansion in the volume of attacks occurs after 2005, driven largely by increases in the Middle East & North Africa and South Asia bands. By 2014, the Middle East & North Africa region forms the widest band in the stack, reaching a height that surpasses all other individual regions, while Sub-Saharan Africa also shows a visible increase in thickness during this period compared to the 1990s. Following the 2014 peak, the total area contracts, with the Middle East & North Africa band narrowing considerably by 2021, though it remains a substantial portion of the total. South Asia maintains a consistent presence throughout the three decades, showing a rise in the mid-2000s and a subsequent decrease, yet it stays above the levels seen in Eastern Europe or Southeast Asia. The lowest bands throughout the entire period belong to Central Asia, Eastern Europe, and Australasia & Oceania, which appear as thin lines near the horizontal axis. The data shows a concentration of activity in specific geographic areas during the mid-2010s, followed by a reduction in the total count across most regions toward the end of the observed period.
